questions: You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. Your company introduces a new dress code policy that you find unnecessarily restrictive. How do you respond?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. Your school administration bans a book from the library that you think students should read. What do you do?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. A new HOA rule prohibits decorating your front yard with anything not on an approved list. How do you handle it?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. Your boss tells you to follow a new process that you believe makes your work worse but makes management's reporting easier. What do you do?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. A local ordinance restricts when you can use your backyard fire pit based on complaints from one neighbor. How do you react?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. Your university requires attendance at a mandatory workshop you think is pointless. What do you do?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. Traffic laws in your area seem designed more for revenue than safety. How do you drive?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. Your professional licensing board requires continuing education that you believe is outdated and irrelevant. How do you comply?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. A social convention dictates how you should dress, speak, or behave at a family gathering you attend regularly. What do you do?

You're someone who respects institutions and works within established systems, believing rules exist for good reason. A streaming service you use changes their terms of service in a way that limits how you use content you've already paid for. How do you respond?
